gate0.source_grade_rationale: The Motley Fool is a secondary investment commentary outlet. The underlying primary event — the Bank of America analyst note by Vivek Arya — is Grade A, but the signal was ingested from the secondary report.

materiality_math: Base 55 (material relevance, guidance_change + macro_sector + product events, primary_issuer linkage, sell-side analyst call with 6% same-day stock move; Grade B source, not an official filing) + novelty 0.85 × 20 = +17 − penalties 0 (new_event, no duplicate) + overrides 0 (no official filing, enforcement, or default) = 72/100
routing_reason: Materiality 72 clears the 70-point PROMOTE threshold. New analyst call shifting Intel consensus view: two-notch upgrade (underperform to buy), 41% price target increase ($96 to $135), and a TAM revision ($125B to $170B+ server CPU market by 2030) tied to agentic AI demand. High novelty (0.85) and primary issuer linkage. No prior Intel or BofA analyst event in the 48-hour ledger window.

sources.claim_supported: Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya double-upgraded Intel from underperform to buy on 2026-06-11, raising price target from $96 to $135; server CPU TAM revised $125B to $170B+ by 2030; Intel Q1 2026 data-center and AI revenue ~$5.1B (+22% YoY); shares +6% on the day. [Unverified secondary — primary BofA research note not directly in ledger]
